title: galeopsis ladanum proles reuteri
intro: Galeopsis ladanum proles reuteri, also known as Reuter's knotgrass, is a rare and lesser-known subspecies of the Galeopsis ladanum plant, a member of the mint family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. Reuter's knotgrass has been found to have several health benefits, including relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as reducing inflammation and improving digestion. Herbal preparations of Reuter's knotgrass are often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and it can also be found in some herbal supplements.
